data.title: Canada Needs to Clear Away Hurdles to Energy Industry Investment, Enbridge CEO Says
data.description:
By Robb M. Stewart


There is an urgency for Canada to tap its energy resources to grow its economy and compete internationally, but the country won't attract the needed capital unless regulatory changes are made, Enbridge Chief Executive Greg Ebel warned.

The head of the energy infrastructure company said the country faces a defining moment as the world rewires supply chains, and it has an opportunity to make the most of a resources advantage.

"Energy security is now synonymous with national and economic security. Capital is flowing toward countries that can move with certainty and speed," Ebel said in a speech in Toronto to the nonprofit Empire Club of Canada.

His comments came the same week that the province of Alberta's government said it would back and help develop a proposed oil pipeline to Canada's northwest coast with an industry advisory group that includes Enbridge, South Bow and Trans Mountain. Prime Minister Mark Carney has also pushed a goal of making the country an energy superpower as Canada pivots toward global markets and away from a reliance on the U.S.

Expansion of Canada's energy industry requires regulatory processes that will move more quickly, Ebel said. He said major project approval in Canada can take five years or more, which is a competitive liability.

Ebel said there was no call to weaken environmental oversight, but instead processes need to be more predictable.

He also said the country is held back by regulations, including energy-industry carbon taxes and emissions caps. Ebel also pointed to the ban on oil tankers that Ottawa introduced almost a decade ago along a segment of the northern British Columbia coast, which effectively derailed Enbridge's proposed Northern Gate pipeline project.

"The last 10 years have been a competitiveness disaster for Canada," Ebel said. "The next 10 can be a renaissance, if we choose to lead."

He added energy is a core driver for Canada, contributing more than 10% of gross domestic product and about one-third of total Canadian goods exported.

"Canada has extraordinary resources, a skilled workforce, and leading environmental standards. Yet we aren't moving fast enough," he said. "Projects linger. The permit process stretches on. Capital looks elsewhere."


Write to Robb M. Stewart at robb.stewart@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-02-25 1538ET

data.title: Legrand SA (ENXTPA:LR) agreed to acquire Avtron Power Solutions, LLC at an enterprise value of $1.125 billion.
data.description: Legrand SA (ENXTPA:LR) agreed to acquire Avtron Power Solutions, LLC at an enterprise value of $1.125 billion on October 2, 2025. The transaction is subject to customary closing conditions, including regulatory approvals.

data.title: Teradyne, Inc. Unveils Titan HP
data.description:
Teradyne, Inc. announced the launch of its Titan HP system level test (SLT) platform, designed specifically for the artificial intelligence (AI) and cloud infrastructure markets. This innovative solution addresses the increasing demands of today's advanced technologies as process nodes become smaller and new architectures emerge. SLT has become an integral part of the high-volume manufacturing process, as the complex devices used in AI and cloud infrastructure deployments push the boundaries of power consumption and thermal output.

Teradyne Titan HP, currently in production at a number of large customers, is purpose-built to test these devices in real-world conditions. Currently supporting up to two kilowatts of power, with a roadmap to support four kilowatts in the near future, customers can be assured that their investment will meet the demanding requirements of tomorrow's devices as well. Teradyne Titan HP's superior thermal control ensures devices meet rigorous quality standards at or above the expected yield.

This includes: A multi-branch cold plate architecture, which cools both the device under test (DUT) and non-DUT components for comprehensive thermal management. Asynchronous cooling control with proportional, integral and derivative (PID)-tuned, per-site thermal control, which provides precise, efficient cooling to prevent overheating. A DUT heater option, which enhances the accuracy of automated temperature control (ATC) for more precise thermal testing.

Disney To Refresh App Features As Hulu Integration Continues
data.description:
By Josh Beckerman


Disney said Hulu will become the global general entertainment brand on Disney+, replacing Star in international markets.

The integration is official Oct. 8, continuing Disney's move to phase out Hulu as a standalone app.

"With this change, and in preparation for a fully integrated unified app experience next year, users will begin to see further integrations of Hulu across the Disney+ app as part of an ongoing and iterative slate of product updates on the homepage and beyond," the company said.

Disney this year purchased Hulu outright after buying Comcast's stake in the streaming platform. In August, Disney said it would take a major step forward in strengthening its streaming offering by fully integrating Hulu into Disney+. At the time, it said the replacement of the Star tile internationally would take place in the fall.

Disney is also making cosmetic and user-friendly additions as part of the Hulu integration, with a new navigation bar with tabs for Disney+, Hulu, and ESPN, depending on the user's subscription. What's more, the "For You" tab is becoming the first screen subscribers see when they open Disney+.

"We've been hard at work behind the scenes to evolve and advance our personalized video algorithm that will learn and improve over time," the company said. Disney+ will also make profiles more prominent to ensure users are watching content in their own profile, the company said.


Write to Josh Beckerman at josh.beckerman@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-02-25 1514ET

Sector Update: Consumer
Corn Futures Rise as Traders Set Aside Uncertainty -- Daily Grain Highlights
data.description:
By Kirk Maltais


--Corn for December delivery rose 1.1% to $4.21 1/2 a bushel on the Chicago Board of Trade Thursday, with President Trump's assertions of another meeting with Chinese President Xi within the next four weeks.

--Wheat for December delivery rose 1% to $5.14 3/4 a bushel.

--Soybeans for November delivery rose 0.9% to $10.22 1/2 a bushel.


HIGHLIGHTS


Shrugging Off the Negative: CBOT grains appeared to ride residual momentum from President Trump's Truth Social post yesterday saying he'll meet with China's President Xi in 4 weeks. Outside of that, factors moving the market appear more negative. "Sort of strange action as most of the inputs are bearish but all markets trading higher today," said Joel Karlin of Ocean State Research--adding that many traders are reluctant to attach much hope to Trump's post. Multiple firms released their forecasts for U.S. crop yields and production, showing reductions from the USDA's September WASDE estimates for corn but still at record highs.

Risky Business: Rising geopolitical volatility in the Black Sea supported higher wheat futures. Russia is a primary supplier of wheat throughout the world, and Ukraine also has a sizable agricultural output, although that's been hindered by war in recent years. Lifting wheat today is a Russian strike hitting Ukrainian targets, while resumed help for Ukraine from the U.S. appears to make harder Ukrainian counterstrikes possible. "Black Sea risk is gradually escalating," said Andrey Sizov of SovEcon in a note. Comments from President Trump about moving "nuclear" submarines closer to Russia also ratcheted up the tension.

Pledging Support: Soybean futures were lower in premarket trading, before turning higher as the day progressed. In an appearance on CNBC, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told interviewers that "you should expect some news on Tuesday on substantial support for the farmers." President Trump posted on Truth Social yesterday that he was working to support farmers struggling with China's absence from buying U.S. soybean exports, and also said that he would be meeting with Chinese President Xi on trade within the next four weeks.


INSIGHT


Case Sensitive: Analysts are watching for "how sensitive prices will be to news or comments surrounding Chinese demand, or lack thereof," said ADM Investor Service in a note. The firm said that without resumed Chinese purchasing, the USDA's current projections for soybean export sales are "optimistic." Other analysts note that China has made a lot of its normal export purchases already, with Brazil and other competitors.

Stubbornly Large: Research firm StoneX released new estimates regarding U.S. crop yields. The firm said that it now forecasts corn yields to drop from the USDA's previous estimate of 186.7 bushels an acre to 185.9 bushels an acre. StoneX also raised its outlook for soybean yields to 53.9 bushels an acre, up 0.4 bushels an acre from the USDA's last estimate. The ongoing government shutdown may delay the October WASDE report if it becomes an extended shutdown. "The bottom line though is that both of these yields keep the markets well supplied," said Arlan Suderman of StoneX in a note.


AHEAD


Note: The government shutdown may impact the timing of USDA reports and data releases.

--The USDA is scheduled to release its weekly Grain Export Inspections report at 11 a.m. ET Monday.

--The USDA is scheduled to release its weekly Crop Progress report at 4 p.m. ET Monday.


Write to Kirk Maltais at kirk.maltais@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-02-25 1511ET

RPM International Inc. Declares Regular Quarterly Cash Dividend, Payable on October 31, 2025
data.description: RPM International Inc. announced at its annual meeting of stockholders that its board of directors declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.54 per share, payable on October 31, 2025, to stockholders of record as of October 20, 2025. This payment represents a 5.9% increase over the $0.51 quarterly cash dividend paid at this time last year and is the 52nd consecutive year the company has increased its cash dividend.
